content.title: Recognising Characters in Simple Signs
content.body:
HSK 1 texts often appear on signs, notices, and simple labels. You should be able to recognise common characters like 人 (person), 出口 (exit), 入口 (entrance), 不 (not), 是 (is), 有 (have), and 在 (at/in).

When reading, identify each character first, then combine them to understand the meaning. For example

content.title: Reading Courtesy Expressions
content.body:
Polite expressions in text:
- 请 (please)
- 谢谢 (thank you)
- 对不起 (sorry)
- 没关系 (it's okay/no problem)
- 再见 (goodbye)

These may appear in dialogues or signs.

content.title: Reading Time-Based Sequences
content.body:
Time words create sequences:
- 昨天 (yesterday) → past
- 今天 (today) → present
- 明天 (tomorrow) → future

Multiple time references may appear in one text.

content.title: Understanding Basic Questions in Text
content.body:
Written questions use interrogative words you've learned:
- 什么 (what)
- 谁 (who)
- 哪 (which)
- 几 (how many)
- 吗 (question particle)

Questions with 吗 are yes/no questions. Questions with specific question words ask for information.

content.title: Reading Currency and Shopping
content.body:
Money expressions use:
- 钱 (money)
- 块 (yuan/dollar, measure word)
- 多少 (how much/many)

Example: 多少钱？(how much money?), 五块钱 (five yuan).

content.title: Reading a Family Description
content.body:
我有一个爸爸，一个妈妈，和一个弟弟。我的爸爸是医生。我的妈妈是老师。我的弟弟是学生。我也是学生。

How many people are students?

a: One
b: Two
c: Three
d: Four

answer: b

content.title: Reading a Simple Notice
content.body:
商店今天不开。明天九点开门。

When is the store open?

a: Today
b: Tomorrow at 9
c: Every day at 9
d: Not stated

answer: b

content.title: Interpreting Questions and Answers
content.body:
Match questions to appropriate answers:
- 什么 questions → specific items
- 谁 questions → people
- 几/多少 questions → numbers/quantities
- 哪 questions → choices
- 吗 questions → yes/no responses

content.title: Reading a Simple Dialogue Exchange
content.body:
A: 你好！你叫什么名字？
B: 我叫王明。你呢？
A: 我叫李美。你是学生吗？
B: 是的，我是学生。你也是学生吗？
A: 不是，我是老师。

Who is the teacher?

a: Wang Ming
b: Li Mei
c: Both
d: Neither

answer: b

content.title: Reading a Simple Introduction
content.body:
我叫张小明。我是中国人。我是学生。我有一个老师，她叫李老师。我喜欢看书。

Which statement is correct?

a: Zhang Xiaoming is a teacher
b: Zhang Xiaoming is Chinese
c: Li is Zhang Xiaoming's student
d: Zhang Xiaoming doesn't like books

answer: b

content.title: Understanding Negation in Context
content.body:
Negation changes meaning significantly:
- 不 negates most verbs and adjectives
- 没 negates 有 (have)
- Position of 不/没 matters

Read carefully to catch negative words that reverse meaning.

content.title: Reading Action Verbs in Context
content.body:
Common action verbs include:
- 吃 (eat)
- 喝 (drink)
- 看 (see/look/read)
- 去 (go)
- 来 (come)
- 买 (buy)
- 做 (do/make)

These combine with objects: 吃饭 (eat rice/meal), 喝水 (drink water), 看书 (read books).

content.title: Reading About Shopping
content.body:
我今天去商店。我买三本书。一本书十块钱。我也买一个杯子。杯子五块钱。

How much did the person spend on books?

a: 10 yuan
b: 15 yuan
c: 30 yuan
d: 35 yuan

answer: c

content.title: Reading Time Expressions
content.body:
Time expressions in HSK 1 use numbers with time words. Common patterns include:
- 几点 (what time)
- X点 (X o'clock)
- 今天 (today)
- 明天 (tomorrow)
- 昨天 (yesterday)

Example: 今天三点 means "today at 3 o'clock" (今天 + 三 + 点).

content.title: Comprehensive Text Understanding
content.body:
我叫小王。我是中国学生。我有一个好朋友。她叫小李。她也是学生。我们今天去商店买书。小李买两本书。我买三本书。我们明天在学校看书。

How many books do they buy in total?

a: Two
b: Three
c: Five
d: Six

answer: c

content.title: Reading About Daily Activities
content.body:
今天我七点在家吃饭。八点我去学校。我在学校看书。

What does the person do at 8 o'clock?

a: Eat at home
b: Go to school
c: Read at school
d: Go home

answer: b

content.title: Reading Descriptions with 很
content.body:
Adjectives in HSK 1 often appear with 很 (very):
- 很大 (very big)
- 很小 (very small)
- 很好 (very good)
- 很多 (very many/much)
- 很少 (very few/little)

很 links the subject to the adjective: 他很好 (he is very good/fine).

Note: In predicate adjective constructions, 很 is grammatically required and does not alw

content.title: Matching Sentences to Situations
content.body:
When reading HSK 1 texts, match what you read to real situations. Look for:
- WHO is involved (pronouns, names, family terms)
- WHAT is happening (verbs, actions)
- WHERE it happens (location words with 在)
- WHEN it happens (time expressions)

Process of elimination helps when multiple similar optio

content.title: Reading Location Phrases
content.body:
Location phrases use 在 (at/in) followed by a place:
- 在家 (at home)
- 在学校 (at school)
- 在这儿 (here)
- 在那儿 (there)
- 在中国 (in China)

Example: 我在学校 means "I am at school."

content.title: Reading Personal Descriptions
content.body:
Personal descriptions combine multiple elements:
- Name (我叫...)
- Nationality (我是...人)
- Occupation (我是...)
- Family (我有...)
- Preferences (我喜欢...)

content.title: Reading Short Dialogues
content.body:
Simple dialogues show question-answer pairs:
- Speaker A asks a question
- Speaker B responds
- Context clues help identify who says what

Look for question words (什么、谁、几、吗) to identify questions.

content.title: Reading About Preferences and Plans
content.body:
我喜欢喝茶，不喜欢喝咖啡。明天我想去商店买茶。我的朋友也喜欢茶。

What will the person buy tomorrow?

a: Coffee
b: Tea
c: Both tea and coffee
d: Nothing

answer: b

content.title: Understanding Possession and Relationships
content.body:
的 shows possession:
- 我的妈妈 (my mother)
- 他的书 (his book)
- 老师的名字 (teacher's name)

Relationships often use 的 to show connection.

content.title: Reading Multiple Time References
content.body:
昨天我在家看书。今天我去学校。我在学校见我的老师。明天我不去学校。我想在家。

On which day does the person see their teacher?

a: Yesterday
b: Today
c: Tomorrow
d: Every day

answer: b

content.title: Reading Measure Words with Quantities
content.body:
Measure words appear between numbers and nouns:
- 一个人 (one person)
- 三只狗 (three dogs)
- 两本书 (two books)
- 五杯水 (five cups of water)

个 is the general measure word, but specific words exist for certain items.

content.title: Reading Possession Statements
content.body:
Possession uses 有 (have):
- 我有 (I have)
- 他没有 (he doesn't have)
- 你有几个 (how many do you have)

没 negates 有, so 没有 means "don't have" or "there isn't/aren't."

content.title: Reading Location and Existence
content.body:
Distinguish between:
- 在 (located at) → position
- 有 (have/there is) → possession/existence

Example: 学校有老师 (there are teachers at school) vs. 老师在学校 (teacher is at school).

content.title: Reading Wanting and Liking
content.body:
Desire and preference use these verbs:
- 想 (want to/think)
- 要 (want/need)
- 喜欢 (like)

Example: 我想吃 (I want to eat), 他喜欢狗 (he likes dogs).

content.title: Reading People References
content.body:
Family terms appear frequently in simple texts:
- 妈妈 (mom)
- 爸爸 (dad)
- 儿子 (son)
- 女儿 (daughter)
- 老师 (teacher)
- 朋友 (friend)

These combine with verbs like 是 (is), 有 (have), and 在 (at).

questions.question_text:
我的老师是中国人。她的名字叫李老师。
Whose name is Li?

我叫张小明。我是中国人。我是学生。我有一个老师，她叫李老师。我喜欢看书。
What can we confirm about Zhang Xiaoming?

B: 我叫王明。你呢？

B: 是的，我是学生。你也是学生吗？
A: 不是，我是老师。
How does Li Mei ask Wang Ming's name back to her? Write the Chinese phrase.

Read: 我不喜欢猫，我喜欢狗。Which is true?

我昨天在家。我今天在学校。我明天去商店。
Order these locations by when the person is/was/will be there:
What's the difference between 我有朋友在学校 and 我的朋友在学校？

我叫小王。我是中国学生。我有一个好朋友。她叫小李。她也是学生。我们今天去商店买书。小李买两本书。我买三本书。我们明天在学校看书。
Where will they read tomorrow?

What is the total spent?
Read: 这个十块钱。What is the price?

今天我七点在家吃饭。八点我去学校。我在学校看书。
Where does the person read?
Where is the person? 我不在那儿。
Read: 他昨天没有书，今天有书。When did he get the book?

我喜欢喝茶，不喜欢喝咖啡。明天我想去商店买茶。我的朋友也喜欢茶。
What does the friend like?
Read: 我有两本书。How many books?

我有一个儿子和一个女儿。我的儿子是医生。

What does 我有三个 mean?

李医生今天在家，不在医院。

Read: 商店里有很多人。What does this tell us?

我喜欢喝茶，不喜欢喝咖啡。明天我想去商店买茶。我的朋友也喜欢茶。
Which statements are true?
What does the sign 出口 mean?

A: 你叫什么名字？
B: 我叫李明。
What information did B provide?
In 三只狗, what is 只?
What does 我妈妈 mean?

Q: 你有几个孩子？
A: 我有三个孩子。
What did the question ask about?

昨天我在家看书。今天我去学校。我在学校见我的老师。明天我不去学校。我想在家。
Where will the person be tomorrow?
Someone asks: 这是谁的书？Which answer is appropriate?
What does 多少钱 ask?
Read: 他没有书。What doesn't he have?
Read: 他今天不在。When is he not here?

我今天去商店。我买三本书。一本书十块钱。我也买一个杯子。杯子五块钱。
What did the person buy?
Read: 她想去中国。What does she want?
What does 看书 mean?
Read: 他的老师是中国人。What is his teacher?
What does 很大 mean?
What does 他在家 mean?
Which question asks "how many"?

我叫张小明。我是中国人。我是学生。我有一个老师，她叫李老师。我喜欢看书。

What does 谢谢 mean?
Read: 我的狗很小。How is the dog described?

我叫小王。我是中国学生。我有一个好朋友。她叫小李。她也是学生。我们今天去商店买书。小李买两本书。我买三本书。我们明天在学校看书。
